047|static int
048|is_rwm (const char *str, libcrun_error_t *err)
049|{
050|  const char *it;
051|  bool r = false;
052|  bool w = false;
053|  bool m = false;
054|
055|  for (it = str; *it; it++)
056|    switch (*it)
057|      {
058|      case 'r':
059|        r = true;
060|        break;
061|
062|      case 'w':
063|        w = true;
064|        break;
065|
066|      case 'm':
067|        m = true;
068|        break;
069|
070|      default:
071|        return crun_make_error (err, 0, "invalid mode specified `%s`", str);
072|      }
073|
074|  return r && w && m ? 1 : 0;
075|}

077|static int
078|check_cgroup_v2_controller_available_wrapper (int ret, int cgroup_dirfd, const char *name, libcrun_error_t *err)
079|{
080|  if (ret == 0 || err == NULL)
081|    return 0;
082|
083|  errno = crun_error_get_errno (err);
084|
085|  /* If the file is not found, try to give a more meaningful error message.  */
086|  if (errno == ENOENT || errno == EPERM || errno == EACCES)
087|    {
088|      cleanup_free char *controllers = NULL;
089|      libcrun_error_t tmp_err = NULL;
090|      cleanup_free char *key = NULL;
091|      char *saveptr = NULL;
092|      bool found = false;
093|      const char *token;
094|      char *it;
095|
096|      /* Check if the specified controller is enabled.  */
097|      key = xstrdup (name);
098|
099|      it = strchr (key, '.');
100|      if (it == NULL)
101|        {
102|          crun_error_release (err);
103|          return crun_make_error (err, 0, "the specified key has not the form CONTROLLER.VALUE `%s`", name);
104|        }
105|      *it = '\0';
106|
107|      /* cgroup. files are not part of a controller.  Return the original error.  */
108|      if (strcmp (key, "cgroup") == 0)
109|        return ret;
110|
111|      /* If the cgroup.controllers file cannot be read, return the original error.  */
112|      if (read_all_file_at (cgroup_dirfd, "cgroup.controllers", &controllers, NULL, &tmp_err) < 0)
113|        {
114|          crun_error_release (&tmp_err);
115|          return ret;
116|        }
117|      for (token = strtok_r (controllers, " \n", &saveptr); token; token = strtok_r (NULL, " \n", &saveptr))
118|        {
119|          if (strcmp (token, key) == 0)
120|            {
121|              found = true;
122|              break;
123|            }
124|        }
125|      if (! found)
126|        {
127|          crun_error_release (err);
128|          return crun_make_error (err, 0, "the requested cgroup controller `%s` is not available", key);
129|        }
130|    }
131|  return ret;

207|  size_t len;
208|  int ret;
209|
210|  if (blkio->weight)
211|    {
212|      uint32_t val = blkio->weight;
213|
214|      len = sprintf (fmt_buf, "%" PRIu32, val);
215|      if (! cgroup2)

227|                {
228|                  crun_error_release (err);
229|
230|                  /* convert linearly from [10-1000] to [1-10000] */
231|                  val = 1 + (val - 10) * 9999 / 990;

233|                  len = sprintf (fmt_buf, "%" PRIu32, val);
234|

270|                return crun_make_error (err, errno, "write io.weight");

279|          size_t i;
280|
281|          w_device_fd = openat (dirfd, "blkio.weight_device", O_WRONLY);

285|          w_leafdevice_fd = openat (dirfd, "blkio.leaf_weight_device", O_WRONLY);

288|
289|          for (i = 0; i < blkio->weight_device_len; i++)
290|            {
291|              len = sprintf (fmt_buf, "%" PRIu64 ":%" PRIu64 " %" PRIu16 "\n", blkio->weight_device[i]->major,

426|static int
427|write_devices_resources_v1 (int dirfd, runtime_spec_schema_defs_linux_device_cgroup **devs, size_t devs_len,
428|                            libcrun_error_t *err)
429|{
430|  size_t i, len;
431|  int ret;
432|  char *default_devices[] = {
433|    "c *:* m",
434|    "b *:* m",
435|    "c 1:3 rwm",
436|    "c 1:8 rwm",
437|    "c 1:7 rwm",
438|    "c 5:0 rwm",
439|    "c 1:5 rwm",
440|    "c 1:9 rwm",
441|    "c 5:1 rwm",
442|    "c 136:* rwm",
443|    "c 5:2 rwm",
444|    NULL
445|  };
446|
447|  for (i = 0; i < devs_len; i++)
448|    {
449|      /* It is plenty of room for "TYPE MAJOR:MINOR ACCESS", where type is one char, and ACCESS is at most 3.   */
450|#define FMT_BUF_LEN 64
451|      char fmt_buf[FMT_BUF_LEN];
452|      const char *file = devs[i]->allow ? "devices.allow" : "devices.deny";
453|
454|      if (devs[i]->type == NULL || devs[i]->type[0] == 'a')
455|        {
456|          strcpy (fmt_buf, "a");
457|          len = 1;
458|        }
459|      else
460|        {
461|          char fmt_buf_major[16];
462|          char fmt_buf_minor[16];
463|
464|#define FMT_DEV(x, b)               \
465|  do                                \
466|    {                               \
467|      if (x##_present)              \
468|        sprintf (b, "%" PRIi64, x); \
469|      else                          \
470|        strcpy (b, "*");            \
471|  } while (0)
472|
473|          FMT_DEV (devs[i]->major, fmt_buf_major);
474|          FMT_DEV (devs[i]->minor, fmt_buf_minor);
475|
476|          len = snprintf (fmt_buf, FMT_BUF_LEN - 1, "%s %s:%s %s", devs[i]->type, fmt_buf_major, fmt_buf_minor,
477|                          devs[i]->access);
478|          /* Make sure it is still a NUL terminated string.  */
479|          fmt_buf[len] = '\0';
480|        }
481|      ret = write_cgroup_file (dirfd, file, fmt_buf, len, err);
482|      if (UNLIKELY (ret < 0))
483|        return ret;
484|    }
485|
486|  for (i = 0; default_devices[i]; i++)
487|    {
488|      ret = write_cgroup_file (dirfd, "devices.allow", default_devices[i], strlen (default_devices[i]), err);
489|      if (UNLIKELY (ret < 0))
490|        return ret;
491|    }
492|
493|  return 0;
494|}

496|static int
497|write_devices_resources_v2_internal (int dirfd, runtime_spec_schema_defs_linux_device_cgroup **devs, size_t devs_len,
498|                                     libcrun_error_t *err)
499|{
500|  int i, ret;
501|  cleanup_free struct bpf_program *program = NULL;
502|  struct default_dev_s
503|  {
504|    char type;
505|    int major;
506|    int minor;
507|    const char *access;
508|  };
509|  struct default_dev_s default_devices[] = {
510|    { 'c', -1, -1, "m" },
511|    { 'b', -1, -1, "m" },
512|    { 'c', 1, 3, "rwm" },
513|    { 'c', 1, 8, "rwm" },
514|    { 'c', 1, 7, "rwm" },
515|    { 'c', 5, 0, "rwm" },
516|    { 'c', 1, 5, "rwm" },
517|    { 'c', 1, 9, "rwm" },
518|    { 'c', 5, 1, "rwm" },
519|    { 'c', 136, -1, "rwm" },
520|    { 'c', 5, 2, "rwm" },
521|  };
522|
523|  program = bpf_program_new (2048);
524|
525|  program = bpf_program_init_dev (program, err);
526|  if (UNLIKELY (program == NULL))
527|    return -1;
528|
529|  for (i = (sizeof (default_devices) / sizeof (default_devices[0])) - 1; i >= 0; i--)
530|    {
531|      program = bpf_program_append_dev (program, default_devices[i].access, default_devices[i].type,
532|                                        default_devices[i].major, default_devices[i].minor, true, err);
533|      if (UNLIKELY (program == NULL))
534|        return -1;
535|    }
536|  for (i = devs_len - 1; i >= 0; i--)
537|    {
538|      char type = 'a';
539|      int minor = -1, major = -1;
540|      if (devs[i]->type != NULL)
541|        type = devs[i]->type[0];
542|
543|      if (devs[i]->major_present)
544|        major = devs[i]->major;
545|      if (devs[i]->minor_present)
546|        minor = devs[i]->minor;
547|
548|      program = bpf_program_append_dev (program, devs[i]->access, type, major, minor, devs[i]->allow, err);
549|      if (UNLIKELY (program == NULL))
550|        return -1;
551|    }
552|
553|  program = bpf_program_complete_dev (program, err);
554|  if (UNLIKELY (program == NULL))
555|    return -1;
556|
557|  ret = libcrun_ebpf_load (program, dirfd, NULL, err);
558|  if (ret < 0)
559|    return ret;
560|
561|  return 0;

566|                            libcrun_error_t *err)

577|  for (i = 0; i < devs_len; i++)
